perturbation.original_text: Reference is hereby made to that certain fully executed Wireless Content License Agreement Number 12965 dated as of December 16, 2004 ("Agreement"), between TWENTIETH CENTURY FOX LICENSING & MERCHANDISING, division of Fox Entertainment Group, Inc. ("Fox"), as Administrator for Twentieth Century Fox Film Corporation ("Trademark Licensor") and Glu Mobile, Inc. f/k/a Sorrent, Inc. ("Licensee").
perturbation.changed_text: Reference is hereby made to that certain fully executed Wireless Content License Agreement Number 12965 dated as of December 16, 2004 ("Agreement"), between TWENTIETH CENTURY FOX LICENSING & MERCHANDISING, division of Fox Entertainment Group, Inc. ("Fox"), as Administrator for Twentieth Century Fox Film Corporation ("Trademark Licensor") and Glu Mobile, Inc. f/k/a Sorrent, Inc. ("User").
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Licensee' is changed to 'User.' This change creates a contradiction later in the document where 'Licensee' is used to define specific rights and responsibilities. By changing it to 'User', we create ambiguity as to who those rights and responsibilities are assigned.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (ii) "ICE AGE 2" GUARANTEE AND ROYALTY: In addition to any outstanding Guarantee payments which Licensee shall pay to Fox as set forth in Paragraph 3 of this Amendment below, Licensee shall pay to Fox a minimum recoupable guarantee of ***** dollars (US$*****) on or before ***** ("ICE AGE 2 Guarantee"), and ***** The omitted portions of this exhibit have been filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission pursuant to a request for confidential treatment under Rule 406 promulgated under the Securities Act of 1933. Source: GLU MOBILE INC, S-1/A, 3/19/2007 Fox shall earn, and Licensee shall pay the applicable Major Release Royalties as set forth in Paragraph 7(a)(i) of the Agreement in connection with the property "ICE AGE 2". For the avoidance of doubt, there shall be no cross- collateralization of the Royalties collected from Licensee's sale, license, distribution or other exploitation of the Wireless Products derived from the Property "ICE AGE 2", with the Royalties collected from any other Wireless Products released by Licensee under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Licensee' to 'User' creates a direct contradiction. The contradicted text explicitly states 'Licensee shall pay to Fox'. By redefining 'Licensee' as 'User', the obligation to pay royalties becomes ambiguous. This aligns with the Misaligned Terminology definition, creating ambiguity and thus a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Licensee shall develop the following Wireless Product in conjunction with "In Her Shoes": one (1) 2-D casual-style puzzle game. ("IN HER SHOES Wireless Product")
perturbation.changed_text: User shall develop the following Wireless Product in conjunction with "In Her Shoes": one (1) 2-D casual-style puzzle game. ("IN HER SHOES Wireless Product")
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Licensee' to 'User' in this section introduces ambiguity about which entity is responsible for developing the wireless product. This directly contradicts the overall agreement which designates the 'Licensee' with development responsibilities.
perturbation.contradicted_text: MUTUTAL RELEASE: With the exception of Licensee's Royalties payment obligations with respect to all Wireless Products released as of the date of this Amendment, and the obligations set forth in this Amendment, neither party shall have any further obligation under the Agreement, and all claims arising under the Agreement shall be mutually released. ***** The omitted portions of this exhibit have been filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission pursuant to a request for confidential treatment under Rule 406 promulgated under the Securities Act of 1933. Source: GLU MOBILE INC, S-1/A, 3/19/2007
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Licensee' to 'User' introduces ambiguity regarding the party responsible for developing the 'IN HER SHOES Wireless Product'. The contradicted text refers to 'Licensee's Royalties payment obligations with respect to all Wireless Products'. If 'User' and 'Licensee' are not the same entity, it is unclear who is responsible for royalty payments, thus creating a contradiction and uncertainty regarding obligations.

perturbation.original_text: At such time that ***** percent (*****%) of the Guarantee Forfeiture Payment is recouped by Licensee, Fox shall earn and Licensee shall pay to Fox Royalties at the rate of ***** percent (*****%) of Licensee's gross receipts from Licensee's sale, license, distribution or other exploitation of the IN HER SHOES Wireless Product.
perturbation.changed_text: At such time that ***** percent (*****%) of the Guarantee Forfeiture Payment is recouped by User, Fox shall earn and 'Designated Third Party' shall pay to Fox Royalties at the rate of ***** percent (*****%) of Designated Third Party gross receipts from Designated Third Party sale, license, distribution or other exploitation of the IN HER SHOES Wireless Product.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Licensee' with 'Designated Third Party' and User in this paragraph creates confusion around who is responsible for paying royalties. In the original text, it is clear that the 'Licensee' pays. Now it is 'Designated Third Party'. Furthermore using the term User to recoup payment, introduces a contradiction, since the term User hasn't been properly addressed in the document.
perturbation.contradicted_text: DISTRIBUTION FEE FOR DIRECT-TO-CONSUMER DISTRIBUTION: Should Fox or its assigns choose to distribute the Wireless Products through Fox's and its assigns' direct-to-consumer distribution channels, Fox or its assigns shall deduct a percentage of Fox's Gross Receipts (defined as monies received by or credited to Fox or its assigns from Fox's or its assigns' direct-to-consumer distribution channels, for the download of the Wireless Products by end users, or the sale or download of Wireless Products to end users) collected from the sale of such Wireless Products ("Distribution Fee") in the amount of ***** percent (*****%) of Fox's Gross Receipts. After Fox or its assigns deducts its Distribution Fee, it shall remit the remainder to Licensee and Licensee shall pay to Fox or its assigns or such other party as Fox or its assigns may designate in writing, Royalties in the amounts set forth in the Agreement or this Amendment.
perturbation.justification: The original text clearly states the 'Licensee' pays Royalties. The changed text replaces 'Licensee' with 'Designated Third Party' and User. This directly contradicts the Contradicted Text, which states 'Licensee shall pay to Fox'. This creates a clear conflict regarding who is responsible for the Royalty payments, leading to uncertainty and potential unenforceability, adhering to the Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction definition provided.
